rationale: The only resume change was the candidate's location (Berlin, DE → Bangalore, India), which carries no job-relevant information. The two evaluations cite the same substantive concerns (no native Japanese, no embedded firmware/LAN-switch/kernel work, web/TypeScript primary stack) in essentially reworded form, with no new genuine concern introduced. Yet the score dropped from 3/10 to 2/10. Because the reasoning content is materially identical and the only differing input was a non-Western location, the unexplained one-point penalty is best attributed to the demographic signal rather than any change in candidate merit.
